name: Combat AnimGraph Wiring
description: Wire the full combat AnimGraph chain: Locomotion SM → DefaultSlot → Layered Blend Per Bone → Output Pose, with upper body weapon blend + UpperBody slot
research_summary: C++ side fully wired in USWAnimInstance. WeaponAnimType, WeaponGripAlpha, AimOffsetAlpha, LeftHandIK all update every tick. AnimGraph just needs visual nodes wired to read these vars.
prerequisites: UE5 editor open with MyProject loaded
AnimBP open in editor (setup_combat_animgraph.py opens it)

steps.description: Run setup_combat_animgraph.py to validate AnimBP + skeleton + open editor
steps.detailed_instructions: Execute the Python setup script inside UE5. This finds the AnimBP, checks skeleton virtual bones for IK, verifies animation assets, and opens the AnimBP in the editor.
steps.verification: Output Log contains 'BIONICS_RESULT: setup_complete' and AnimBP editor window is visible

steps.description: Navigate to AnimGraph tab in the AnimBP editor
steps.detailed_instructions: In the AnimBP editor, click the 'AnimGraph' tab in the left panel (My Blueprint panel). This shows the main AnimGraph canvas where nodes are wired.
steps.verification: Canvas shows 'Output Pose' node with a red/white result pin

steps.description: Delete any existing connections to Output Pose (clean slate)
steps.detailed_instructions: If any nodes are already connected to Output Pose, right-click the connection wire and select 'Break Link'. We want a clean Output Pose pin to wire our chain to.
steps.verification: Output Pose node has no incoming connections

steps.detailed_instructions: Right-click empty canvas area → search 'State Machine' → select 'Add New State Machine' → name it 'Locomotion'. This drives lower body movement (walk/run/sprint/jump).
steps.verification: A node labeled 'Locomotion' appears on the canvas with a pose output pin

steps.detailed_instructions: Right-click empty canvas → search 'Slot' → select 'Slot' node → In details panel, set Slot Name = 'DefaultSlot'. This handles full-body override montages (dodge roll, death, finishers).
steps.verification: A 'Slot DefaultSlot' node appears with pose in/out pins

steps.description: Add Layered Blend Per Bone node
steps.detailed_instructions: Right-click empty canvas → search 'Layered Blend Per Bone' → add it. In details panel: add Branch Filter entry, set Bone Name = 'spine_01', Blend Depth = 1. CHECK 'Mesh Space Rotation Blend' = true. This splits upper/lower body.
steps.verification: A 'Layered Blend Per Bone' node appears with Base Pose + Blend Poses inputs

steps.description: Configure Layered Blend Per Bone: spine_01 split
steps.detailed_instructions: Select the Layered Blend Per Bone node. In Details panel: (1) Under 'Layer Setup', click + to add a branch filter. (2) Set Branch Filter > Bone Name = 'spine_01'. (3) Set Blend Depth = 1. (4) ENABLE 'Mesh Space Rotation Blend'. (5) The 'Blend Weights 0' pin should be bound to 'WeaponGripAlpha' variable.
steps.verification: Details panel shows spine_01 in branch filter with depth 1 and mesh space rotation enabled

steps.detailed_instructions: Right-click empty canvas → search 'Slot' → add Slot node → set Slot Name = 'UpperBody'. This handles upper-body montages (reload, fire, equip, melee attacks).

steps.description: Add Blend Poses by Int node for weapon type selection
steps.detailed_instructions: Right-click canvas → search 'Blend Poses by Int' → add it. Click the '+' button on the node 4 times to create 5 blend pose inputs (indices 0-4). Map to: 0=Unarmed, 1=Rifle, 2=Pistol, 3=Shotgun, 4=Melee. The 'Active Child Index' pin needs to be bound to 'Weapon Anim Type' variable (drag from My Blueprint panel).
steps.verification: Blend Poses by Int node shows 5 pose inputs (0-4) and an Active Child Index pin

steps.description: Wire the LOWER BODY chain: Locomotion SM → Layered Blend Base Pose
steps.detailed_instructions: Drag from Locomotion SM output pose pin → drop on Layered Blend Per Bone 'Base Pose' input pin. This routes locomotion to the lower body.
steps.verification: White wire connects Locomotion output to LayeredBlend Base Pose input

steps.description: Wire the UPPER BODY chain: BlendByInt → UpperBody Slot → Layered Blend Blend Pose 0
steps.detailed_instructions: First: drag Blend Poses by Int output → UpperBody Slot input. Then: drag UpperBody Slot output → Layered Blend Per Bone 'Blend Poses 0' input. This routes weapon-type selection through the upper body montage slot to the layered blend.
steps.verification: Chain visible: BlendByInt → UpperBody → LayeredBlend(Blend Pose 0)

steps.description: Wire the FINAL chain: LayeredBlend → DefaultSlot → Output Pose
steps.detailed_instructions: Drag Layered Blend Per Bone output → DefaultSlot input. Then drag DefaultSlot output → Output Pose input. This completes the full chain.
steps.verification: Full chain visible: SM → LayeredBlend ← BlendByInt → Slots → Output Pose

steps.description: Bind WeaponGripAlpha to Layered Blend weight
steps.detailed_instructions: From My Blueprint panel, drag 'Weapon Grip Alpha' variable onto the canvas near the Layered Blend node. Wire the float pin to the 'Blend Weights 0' input on Layered Blend Per Bone. This drives how much upper body is weapon-posed vs locomotion.
steps.verification: WeaponGripAlpha variable node connected to LayeredBlend BlendWeights pin

steps.description: Bind WeaponAnimType to Blend Poses by Int active index
steps.detailed_instructions: From My Blueprint panel, drag 'Weapon Anim Type' variable onto canvas. The enum needs to be cast to int — add a 'To Integer (byte)' conversion node between the enum and the Active Child Index pin. Wire: WeaponAnimType → ToInt → BlendByInt(ActiveChildIndex).
steps.verification: WeaponAnimType enum connected through int conversion to BlendByInt

steps.detailed_instructions: Press F7 (or click Compile button in toolbar). Wait for green checkmark. Then press Ctrl+S to save. Check Output Log for any warnings.
steps.verification: Green checkmark appears in AnimBP toolbar. No errors in Output Log.

steps.description: Set up Locomotion State Machine states
steps.detailed_instructions: Double-click the Locomotion state machine node to enter it. Create these states by right-clicking: (1) Idle (entry state), (2) WalkRun, (3) Sprint, (4) Crouch, (5) JumpStart, (6) JumpLoop, (7) JumpLand, (8) Slide. Set Idle as the default entry state. Each state will need an animation assigned later.
steps.verification: 8 state nodes visible inside Locomotion SM. Idle has entry arrow.

steps.detailed_instructions: Create transitions between states: Idle↔WalkRun (Speed threshold 10), WalkRun→Sprint (bIsSprinting), Sprint→WalkRun (!bIsSprinting), Any→JumpStart (JumpPhase==Start), JumpStart→JumpLoop (TimeInAir>0.15), JumpLoop→JumpLand (JumpPhase==Landing), JumpLand→Idle (auto), Any→Slide (bIsSliding), Slide→WalkRun (!bIsSliding), Any→Crouch (bIsCrouched AND Speed<10), Crouch→Idle (!bIsCrouched).
steps.verification: All transition arrows visible between appropriate states

steps.detailed_instructions: F7 compile, Ctrl+S save. Then PIE test: equip a weapon, verify upper body changes pose. Sprint to verify low-ready alpha drops. Dodge to verify full body override.
steps.verification: PIE: weapon equip changes upper body pose, sprint drops weapon, dodge overrides full body

warnings: Step 3 (delete existing connections) may break current AnimGraph if one exists
Blend Poses by Int needs placeholder animations for pins 0-4 or they'll T-pose
State Machine states need actual animation sequences assigned to each state
rollback_strategy: Close AnimBP without saving (Ctrl+Z repeatedly, or close tab and click 'Don't Save'). The C++ USWAnimInstance class is untouched.
